## [12:11 - 15:12]

Now, if we would take the same approach from an email drip to messenger and somebody subscribes to our bot, let's say somebody downloaded our lead magnet, and we're going to subscribe them to a sequence, and we're going to set up a sequence that sends a message every day. Day one, day two, day three, day five, whatever. Then that sequence will, by default, violate terms of service. So, what will happen if you set up a sequence, you say we have five days, five messages, and you, somebody subscribed, or you subscribed somebody to a sequence after they downloaded the lead magnet. Great. Now, the first message is sent, the first message in the sequence is sent 20 hours after the user downloaded the lead magnet. So, you're within the 24 hour plus one window. Great. Now, the user does not interact with your first message of the sequence, whether it is because they didn't like your message, or you didn't have a clear call to action in the message, doesn't really matter, they didn't interact with your message. Now, the 24 hour window passes. Now, you have only one message. That's the plus one to re-engage that user. And secondly, you could use the second message in your sequence.
